text: Totally agree <@UF6RLAL7J> <@U01ULEWACPP>, I def need to work out the idea/“find the fun” first with what I’m familiar with, and then finding the optimal tech stack could come later if I want to productise. I was just wondering if there were better levels of abstraction available, without having to munge moving spatially into the DOM (React) or building the interactivity from scratch using a graphics lib (pixi.js)

text: <@UA14TGLTC> thanks! AR/VR will be one of the avenues I want to pursue, but will probably stick with 2D first to flesh out some ideas. The third dimension seems like unnecessary complexity for now (but maybe 2.5D would slip in)

elements.text: thanks! AR/VR will be one of the avenues I want to pursue, but will probably stick with 2D first to flesh out some ideas. The third dimension seems like unnecessary complexity for now (but maybe 2.5D would slip in)

text: I haven’t used this one but I can imagine that this could be useful: <https://reactflow.dev> It already has all the boring work implemented: zoom, pan, … and nodes. Even if you wouldn’t need wires, I assume you could use it to create custom node types and just arrange them on the canvas. It’s used by this tool, also made by the same agency: <https://datablocks.pro>

attachments.title: React Flow - An open source library for building node based applications.
attachments.text: Highly customizable React.js library for building interactive node-based editors, flow charts and diagrams.
attachments.fallback: React Flow - An open source library for building node based applications.

text: ooo, that is an interesting idea <@U01ULEWACPP>. I’ve come across react flow before, but didn’t think to use it for this situation. Someone else figuring out all the boring stuff was exactly what I wanted :sweat_smile: <https://reactflow.dev/examples/drag-and-drop/|Drag and drop> and <https://reactflow.dev/examples/layouting/|layouting> looks like good building blocks for me. Thanks for the tip! :star2:
